title: Sarepta Therapeutics Expected to Beat Earnings Estimates - Can the Stock Move Higher?
text:
Heading into Q1 2024 earnings, Sarepta Therapeutics was expected to beat analyst estimates based on strong pre-earnings momentum and positive catalysts. The company had built considerable positive sentiment with ELEVIDYS label expansion expectations and continued PMO franchise performance.

Pre-Earnings Sentiment: Analysts and investors were bullish on SRPT heading into Q1 2024 earnings announcement. The company had demonstrated strong execution and had multiple catalysts coming into the quarter.

Label Expansion Catalyst: The June 2024 label expansion approval was on the horizon, expected to drive significant upside. Label expansion would make ELEVIDYS available to over 80% of Duchenne patients, compared to the initial 4-5 year old ambulatory population.

Earnings Delivery: The company ultimately delivered strong Q1 2024 results: EPS of $0.44 vs. $0.02 estimate. Revenue of $413.46 million vs. $373.28 million estimate. Net product revenue growth of 55% year-over-year.

Stock Price Implications: With earnings beat and positive forward guidance, analysts questioned whether the stock could move materially higher. Consensus price targets ranged from $20 to over $30 depending on various ELEVIDYS adoption scenarios.

Key Investor Questions: Whether ELEVIDYS could achieve $2-5 billion in peak sales. How much cannibalization would occur with existing PMO franchise. Timeline to profitability and positive cash flow. Sarepta's Q1 results and guidance provided some answers to these questions.
